Class OutboxHealthIndicator

java.lang.Object
org.springframework.boot.health.contributor.AbstractHealthIndicator
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.spring.OutboxHealthIndicator
All Implemented Interfaces:
HealthContributor, HealthIndicator
public class OutboxHealthIndicator extends AbstractHealthIndicator
Spring Boot Actuator AbstractHealthIndicator that probes the outbox table.

The probe is intentionally cheap: it opens a Connection from the configured DataSource and runs SELECT 1 FROM <schema>.<table> WHERE 1 = 0. The query returns no rows but does require the table to exist and the JDBC user to have SELECT on it, so it exercises both schema presence and permissions without scanning data.

Identifier safety mirrors JdbcOutbox — the schema and table names are validated via TableRef before being interpolated into the SQL string. Any value that does not match the conservative identifier pattern is rejected at construction time.

Reports up() with a table detail on success; reports down() carrying the SQLException cause on failure.

Since:
0.1.0

  • Constructor Details

    • OutboxHealthIndicator

      public OutboxHealthIndicator(DataSource dataSource, String schema, String tableName)
      Creates a new health indicator.
      Parameters:
      dataSource - the DataSource the probe opens connections from; never null.
      schema - the outbox schema, or null/blank for an unqualified table.
      tableName - the outbox table name.
      Since:
      0.1.0
